Muting, Blocking, and Banning Players

  • Muting the Player's Microphone
  • Voice Blocking
  • Banning

Muting the Player's Microphone

Players can mute the Xbox Communicator using a handy button on the peripheral itself. Titles have no implementation requirement here and do not have to support muting from within the game unless so desired.

Voice Blocking

Xbox Live titles must allow the user to block the voice of another user permanently. These titles must respect any voice blocks that the user has set previously for other players in a game. Once set, a voice block is bidirectional; all voice from the locked out player should be disabled and no voice from the muting player should be sent to the locked-out player. Beyond the absence of voice or response, the voice block should be undetectable by the locked-out player.

Banning

Xbox Live titles must not allow players banned from voice to communicate over the voice peripheral. Players should be notified at logon if they have been banned from using the voice peripheral. As a result of the logon process, a user's authorization information will indicate the XONLINE_USER_VOICE_NOT_ALLOWED flag. This occurs as a result of the feedback service or parental control settings, and titles must enforce this setting.
